It happens to everyone. Here are a few things you can do to alleviate your boredom:

   1. Read up on a topic that interests you. It could be something you usually avoid due to how busy you are.
   2. Do some light exercise, like going for a run or stretching.
   3. Find something you’ve been putting off, like cleaning out a closet.
   4. Make a list of things you need to do, as it will give you a sense of accomplishment when you check them off.
   5. Do something your coworkers would be surprised to see you doing.
   6. Carve a woodblock, bake a cake, rewire a lamp, etc. It doesn’t matter what, just take pride in a job well done.
   7. Clear your space of things that clutter your thoughts, like paperwork, emails, and digital notifications. You’ll come back to them later.
   8. Daydream about what work will look like in the future. Feel lucky to not be doing that work and use that energy to solve real problems.
   9. Meditate, this can give you a little energy, help quiet your mind and give you a clearer sense of direction.
   10. Reach out to friends and start a conversation. The idea is to get your brain a change of pace and energy.
   11. Take a few minutes to reorganize your immediate surroundings. This is a great way to clear your mind.
   12. Listen to an audiobook, podcast, or other interesting gem on your computer, smartphone or tablet.
   13. Write poetry or journal.
   14. Spend time with your pet or another animal.
   15. Call a long distance friend you haven’t spoken to in awhile.

It's not unusual to feel bored every once in a while. Here are some ways to make yourself feel better when you're bored, but stay safe at home and take care of yourself:

- Clean up your space. Clutter can be overwhelming and make you feel bad. Organize your room, study area, or general living area by sorting and organizing belongings.

- Restock your pantry. Instead of searching through the kitchen for something to eat or drink, restock the shelves. Organize everything so it's easy to see what you have and easy to reach for.

- Assess your accounts: Is there anything you've signed up for but no longer seem to use or care about? Cancel, change, or delete accounts such as magazines, newspapers and any subscriptions.

- Fix things around the house. Dust blinds, vacuum corners, change light bulbs, etc. Taking care of the things around you can help you take care of yourself.

- Write down your daily to do list and resist the urge to skip or procrastinate.

Make sure you're not bored and tired because you're not sleeping enough, physically active or eating a balanced diet. Set yourself up for success by taking care of your body.
Don't be afraid to laugh at yourself (or out loud). Being able to laugh at yourself is a sign of good mental health.

Remember, boredom can be a perfect time to start a new hobby you've always wanted to try but never did, or catch up on rest. But excessive boredom can be a sign of deeper issues, and it's always good to check in with yourself and how you're feeling, especially if your boredom manifests into sadness or apathy.

Hey! It's understandable to feel bored now and then. Boredom can actually be productive because it's an opportunity to learn something new or find a new hobby. Here are some ideas for how to spend your time to beat the boredom:

1) Go outside: Take a walk or hike. Go to the beach, park, or even just around your neighborhood. Studies have shown that spending time in nature, away from screens, can greatly reduce feelings of depression, anxiety, and boredom.

2) Learn a new language: Apps like Duolingo or Babbel make it easy and fun to learn a new language right from your phone or computer, without signing up for classes or buying books.

3) Start a gratitude journal: Every day, take a few minutes to write down three things you're grateful for. It could be as simple as enjoying a cup of coffee, speaking with a friend, or getting through your to-do list. Gratitude helps increase positive outlooks and happiness, and it can take your mind off routine tasks.

4) Try out a new recipe: Bored in the kitchen? Flip through your cookbooks for something you haven't made in a while or look up a healthy recipe online.

5) Start a favorite hobby: Is there a hobby you've put off pursuing because of work or family commitments? Now's a great time to break it out.

6) Connect with friends: Boredom is contagious! If you're bored, your friends may be too. So grab the phone or computer, send a message or hang out on a video chat.

Think about how you usually quit a boring activity, and do the opposite. If you usually stop reading after 10 minutes, tell yourself you will keep reading for an hour. If you usually browse aimlessly, practice focusing for 15 minutes.

I hope these tips help and you feel more engaged and stimulated. Let me know if you'd like any more tips or have any other questions!
